frozen_components: token embedding matrix (except <pause> in Stage II)
architecture.summary: SPOT compresses explicit chain-of-thought reasoning into compact latent <pause> tokens that carry intermediate computation in hidden space. The system uses a teacher–student setup with a frozen pretrained LM backbone. During training, explicit reasoning spans are randomly replaced with <pause> tokens, and the student's <pause> hidden state is aligned to the full span of teacher states via Sinkhorn optimal transport, keeping representations compatible with the frozen LM head for interpretability. At inference, <pause> tokens can be inserted externally at controlled intervals to trade off reasoning length and accuracy without enforcing a fixed template.
trained_components: student model parameters via LoRA (attention and MLP projections)
<pause> token embedding (unfrozen in Stage II only)
span-level alignment objective via Sinkhorn OT

biblio.title: SPOT: Span-level Pause-of-Thought for Efficient and Interpretable Latent Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.dataset: average across GSM8K, MATH500, AIME 2024, AIME 2025, GPQA-Diamond
claims.metric: Pass@1 accuracy and token count reduction
claims.statement: SPOT improves accuracy by 2.3 points on average while reducing generated tokens by 37.5%
On GSM8K, SPOT-stage2 achieves 92.72% accuracy (+3.1 points) while reducing output length by 52.1%
On MATH500, SPOT-stage2 achieves 93.80% accuracy (+1.4 points) while reducing output length by 43.0%
On AIME 2025, SPOT-stage2 achieves 39.33% accuracy (+3.3 points) while generating 15.8% fewer tokens
On GPQA-Diamond (out-of-domain), SPOT-stage2 achieves 54.55% accuracy (+4.5 points) with 49.3% reduction in output length

Sinkhorn-regularized optimal transport alignment objective substantially outperforms endpoint-only (End_KL) and pooled-average (MSE) alternatives, achieving stronger accuracy–length trade-offs across math and science QA benchmarks

Frozen-head decoding constraint keeps latent <pause> states directly decodable under the pretrained LM head, enabling readable keyword interpretations of latent thoughts without training auxiliary probes

Two-stage training (OT alignment followed by rejection-sampled fine-tuning) enables controllable implicit reasoning intensity at inference by externally inserting <pause> tokens without enforcing a fixed response template
